symptom: progressive lopsided overgrowth since childhood with one grotesquely enlarged foot and giant toes
hook: one foot had overgrown grotesquely with massively enlarged bones and giant toes
whatYouSee: A wildly enlarged distorted foot with giant overgrown toes.
whyItMatters: It is a rare mosaic overgrowth disorder that enlarges bones and tissues progressively and asymmetrically and can cause severe deformity and complications.
treatment: Care is supportive and orthopaedic with surgery to debulk or correct disabling overgrowth.
takeaway: Progressive lopsided overgrowth with giant distorted bones and digits suggests Proteus syndrome.

condition.symptom: progressive lopsided overgrowth since childhood with one grotesquely enlarged foot and giant toes
condition.hook: one foot had overgrown grotesquely with massively enlarged bones and giant toes
condition.view: dorsoplantar (AP) radiograph of a single grossly enlarged foot cropped at the ankle with no leg pelvis or groin in frame and one view filling the frame
condition.keyFindings: gross macrodactyly with one or more massively overgrown giant toes two to three times the normal size and greatly enlarged distorted metatarsals and phalanges giving a monstrously enlarged misshapen foot
condition.whatYouSee: A wildly enlarged distorted foot with giant overgrown toes.
condition.whyItMatters: It is a rare mosaic overgrowth disorder that enlarges bones and tissues progressively and asymmetrically and can cause severe deformity and complications.
condition.treatment: Care is supportive and orthopaedic with surgery to debulk or correct disabling overgrowth.
condition.takeaway: Progressive lopsided overgrowth with giant distorted bones and digits suggests Proteus syndrome.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with progressive lopsided overgrowth since childhood with one grotesquely enlarged foot and giant toes.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And one foot had overgrown grotesquely with massively enlarged bones and giant toes.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Proteus syndrome

👀 What you see:
A wildly enlarged distorted foot with giant overgrown toes.

🦴 Why it matters:
It is a rare mosaic overgrowth disorder that enlarges bones and tissues progressively and asymmetrically and can cause severe deformity and complications.

💊 Treatment:
Care is supportive and orthopaedic with surgery to debulk or correct disabling overgrowth.
